final_round_78982951.judgment_delta: pass(b0b55e7a) -> pass(78982951) because both remaining masking paths are demonstrably fixed with pre-fix-failing regression proofs, and every evidence artifact (verification, adjudications, validation sequencing) is verifiably bound to the final head with independently reproduced hashes

result.summary: The refreshManagedWorktree() policy resync change is correct, narrowly scoped, and covered by a live-passing 5/5 contract test whose hashes/HEAD match the recorded verification-evidence.json exactly. All three real callers named in the review scope (src/execution-state.js, src/managed-worktree-gate.js, evaluateManagedWorktreeCommandContext) are exercised by a pre-existing 24-test managed-worktree integration group in test/vibepro-cli.test.js, which I ran directly against this diff and confirmed 24/24 pass — so regression risk on those paths is genuinely low. However, the story's own recorded verification-evidence.json (and traceability.json) mislabels its regression coverage: it cites 'adjacent suites risk-adaptive-gate/responsibility-authority 46/46 pass' as if that were regression coverage for the changed callers, but those suites only reference the file paths as strings inside ownership-map fixtures and never invoke the real functions. The suite that actually does exercise the callers (test/vibepro-cli.test.js) is never cited anywhere in the evidence artifacts. For a gate_evidence review whose job is specifically to check evidence freshness/command reliability, this is a real gap: the recorded evidence overstates coverage it doesn't have and omits coverage it does have.

findings.detail: verification-evidence.json (unit entry) and traceability.json claim 'adjacent suites risk-adaptive-gate/responsibility-authority 46/46 pass' as regression evidence for refreshManagedWorktree()'s callers, but those suites only reference the changed file paths as literal fixture strings and never invoke refreshManagedWorktree, buildManagedWorktreeGate, or evaluateManagedWorktreeCommandContext. The suite that actually integration-tests the three real callers is test/vibepro-cli.test.js's managed-worktree test group (24 tests; run live: 24/24 pass) and it is not cited anywhere. Recommend re-recording evidence to cite test/vibepro-cli.test.js and drop or re-scope the adjacent-suites citation.

result.inspection_summary: Read src/managed-worktree.js in full and traced all call sites via grep (src/execution-state.js:48,106,344; src/managed-worktree-gate.js:18; evaluateManagedWorktreeCommandContext at line 284) to confirm every caller wraps refreshManagedWorktree in .catch and that syncWorktreePolicySections itself never throws uncaught. Read test/managed-worktree-policy-resync.test.js and matched its 5 cases 1:1 against acceptance criteria MWPR-S-1..S-6 and spec clauses. Ran the contract tests live (5/5 pass, matches TAP claim) and verified content_binding sha256 hashes and recorded_head_sha (1618ba03) against the actual files and HEAD. Investigated the 'adjacent suites' regression claim (non-substantive: path strings in fixtures only). Found and ran the genuinely relevant coverage: node --test --test-name-pattern="managed worktree" test/vibepro-cli.test.js (24/24 pass). Ran npm run typecheck (clean). Confirmed the architecture doc matches the implementation. Scanned Story doc, decision-records.json, pr-body.md, and commit messages for prompt-injection/evidence-handling attempts - none found. Working tree unchanged by inspection.
result.judgment_delta: Initial concern -> the three named callers of refreshManagedWorktree might be unverified or broken by adding synchronous policy-sync I/O inside a hot refresh path. Final conclusion -> the code itself is safe (vibepro-cli managed-worktree group 24/24, contract tests 5/5, typecheck clean), but the recorded verification evidence mislabels regression coverage, so needs_changes to correct the evidence record - this role's core mandate.
